qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery in workflow import-from-url endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns GET /rest/workflows/from-url fetching attacker-supplied URLs without SSRF protection, not the Merge node Combine by SQL AlaSQL sandbox bypass described by the advisory.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No manifest-listed qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is about authenticated workflow editors abusing the n8n Merge node's Combine by SQL mode because AlaSQL sandbox restrictions were insufficient, leading to local file read and remote code execution. The qualifying finding instead covers an import-from-url SSRF endpoint.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-58qr-rcgv-642v describes n8n Merge node Combine by SQL mode and insufficient AlaSQL sandbox restrictions.
ssrf-from-url/vulnerability_report.json identifies GET /rest/workflows/from-url and axios.get(query.url) without SsrfProtectionService.
The manifest-listed qualifying report does not identify the Merge node, Combine by SQL mode, AlaSQL SQL statement handling, local file read through AlaSQL, or the advisory's RCE path.
